description: build.gradle.kts is Kotlin code compiled during Gradle configuration and fails with type-safe accessor or plugin errors.
body: Use for build script diagnostics. Do not treat it like Groovy examples from other projects. Follow existing Kotlin DSL syntax and plugin application order. Verify by running a Gradle task that configures the project. Risk boundary is configuration failure before tests run. Use when Kotlin Engineering work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
